text: Machine learning is a type of artificial intelligence where computers learn from data. One common method is supervised learning. In this approach, the computer is given training data, which includes many examples with correct answers. For example, a system might receive thousands of photos labeled "cat" or "dog." The computer uses this data to build a model—a kind of mathematical rule. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. It might guess whether a new photo shows a cat or a dog. The model’s performance is measured by accuracy, which is the percentage of correct predictions.
